summary: Hezbollah FPV drones on fiber optics struck two openly parked Israeli Namer MBTs and a HMMWV armored vehicle, the latter catching fire. Russian forces using the "Rubicon" Center operators recorded over 1,500 strikes on enemy infantry in the open in the past six months, with March 2026 alone seeing 3,170 published episodes—a 40% increase over the previous record and a 5.7-fold rise year-over-year. The "Yolka" anti-drone system intercepted enemy UAVs at distances over 1.5 km, triggering detonations on impact, while Ukrainian forces used drone detectors to survive Russian FPV attacks near Kostiantynivka. Additionally, Russian Ka-52 crews were eliminated after a crash landing near Nadiivka when follow-up drones struck during evacuation.
